title: Fusion LCOE vs. Solar and Wind LCOE (2020-2050)
executive_question: How does fusion cost compare to renewables over time?
required_metrics: Fusion LCOE ($/MWh) - central estimate and range
comparison_set: Fusion vs. solar PV vs. onshore wind
preferred_sources: Lazard Levelized Cost of Energy Analysis
Fusion industry reports (e.g., Fusion Industry Association, company white papers)
search_queries: IEA LCOE projections 2024 solar wind fusion
data_quality_rule: Use official IEA/IRENA/Lazard data for solar and wind. For fusion, use published estimates from credible sources (e.g., MIT, FIA). If only two endpoints exist, mark intermediate years as estimates using CAGR or public forecast benchmarks.
narrative_role: Establishes the cost competitiveness challenge for fusion against established renewables.
pre_exhibit_context: The report argues that fusion will struggle to compete with solar and wind on cost. This exhibit visualizes the projected LCOE trajectories.
post_exhibit_takeaway: Fusion LCOE remains above $50/MWh through 2050, while solar and wind continue to decline, making fusion uncompetitive for grid electricity.

title: Fusion Funding Timeline (2010-2024)
comparison_set: Private vs. public investment over time
data_quality_rule: Use FIA data as primary; cross-check with Crunchbase. If annual data not available, use cumulative and interpolate.
narrative_role: Shows the growth in private and public investment, indicating market confidence.
pre_exhibit_context: The report claims private fusion funding is surging. This exhibit quantifies the trend.
post_exhibit_takeaway: Private fusion funding has grown rapidly, exceeding $5 billion cumulative by 2024, but remains small relative to other energy investments.

title: Fusion Project Pipeline and Milestones
executive_question: What are the key upcoming milestones and their probabilities?
required_metrics: Milestone (e.g., first plasma, Q>1, net energy, grid connection)
data_quality_rule: Use official project announcements. Mark delays explicitly. If probability estimates exist, include them.
narrative_role: Maps the path to commercialization, highlighting key dates and risks.
pre_exhibit_context: The report argues fusion is not imminent. This exhibit shows the timeline of major projects and milestones.
post_exhibit_takeaway: Net energy gain is expected around 2035, but commercial electricity is unlikely before 2050.

title: Tritium Supply vs. Demand for Fusion (2030-2050)
required_metrics: Tritium supply (kg/year) from CANDU reactors and other sources
Tritium breeding rate (kg/year) if technology deployed
preferred_sources: Fusion fuel cycle studies (e.g., from ITER)
search_queries: tritium demand per fusion plant kg/year
data_quality_rule: Use IAEA data as primary. If breeding rates are uncertain, show as a range. Mark intermediate years as estimates.
narrative_role: Identifies a critical supply chain bottleneck that could delay deployment.
pre_exhibit_context: The report highlights tritium breeding as a key challenge. This exhibit quantifies the potential supply gap.
post_exhibit_takeaway: Tritium supply from existing sources is insufficient; breeding technology must be demonstrated at scale to avoid a shortfall.

title: Utility Interest in Fusion: Survey Results
executive_question: How many utilities are actively planning for fusion?
required_metrics: Region: North America, Europe, Asia-Pacific, Rest of World
data_quality_rule: Use published survey results. If no recent survey, note the date and source.
narrative_role: Demonstrates early market demand from potential customers.
pre_exhibit_context: The report claims niche applications will come first. This exhibit shows utility interest levels.
post_exhibit_takeaway: Only a small fraction of utilities have formal fusion plans, indicating early-stage market readiness.

title: Fusion LCOE Sensitivity to Key Variables
executive_question: Which variables most affect fusion cost competitiveness?
required_metrics: Variable: Capital cost ($/kW), Capacity factor (%), Fuel cost ($/MWh), Discount rate (%)
comparison_set: Variables and their impact on LCOE
search_queries: fusion LCOE sensitivity analysis capital cost
data_quality_rule: Use published sensitivity analyses. If not available, create a simple model based on public data and label as estimate.
narrative_role: Shows the impact of key assumptions on LCOE, highlighting uncertainty.
pre_exhibit_context: The report acknowledges high uncertainty in LCOE. This exhibit visualizes sensitivity.
post_exhibit_takeaway: Capital cost and capacity factor are the most influential variables; small changes can make fusion competitive or not.

title: Regulatory Frameworks for Fusion by Country (2024-2030)
executive_question: Which countries are leading in fusion regulation?
required_metrics: Country: USA, UK, Japan, Canada, China, EU
Regulatory stage: None, Draft, Finalized, Enacted
Year of enactment or expected year
preferred_sources: US NRC fusion regulatory framework documents
data_quality_rule: Use official government publications. If stage is uncertain, mark as 'in development'.
narrative_role: Shows the policy landscape and readiness for commercial deployment.
pre_exhibit_context: The report argues regulatory frameworks are needed. This exhibit maps progress.
post_exhibit_takeaway: Only a few countries have established frameworks; most are still developing, creating uncertainty for investors.
